articles.headline: Supreme Court Delivers Landmark Ruling on Data Protection and Digital Privacy
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India today delivered a significant judgment clarifying the contours of the Right to Privacy in the digital age, particularly concerning the implementation of the new Data Protection Act.
• The ruling emphasizes the need for a balanced approach between state surveillance, national security, and individual privacy rights, reiterating that any state intrusion must be proportionate and based on law.
• It sets guidelines for data fiduciaries and processors regarding consent, data minimization, and purpose limitation, impacting digital service providers and government agencies alike.
• Experts believe the judgment will influence the operational aspects of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, and future legislative amendments.
• This decision is expected to strengthen India's position as a proponent of robust data governance while fostering innovation in the digital economy.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Statutory, regulatory and various quasi-judicial bodies.
prelimsPoints: The judgment clarifies the scope of Article 21 (Right to Life and Personal Liberty) concerning digital privacy.
It refers to the Puttaswamy judgment (2017) which declared privacy a fundamental right.
Key provisions of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, are contextualized by the ruling.
The ruling differentiates between public and private data and the level of protection afforded to each.
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of the Supreme Court's ruling on the balance between individual privacy and state security in a digital society.
Discuss how the judgment will shape the implementation of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, and its impact on businesses and citizens.
Evaluate the role of the judiciary in upholding fundamental rights in the face of evolving technological challenges and legislative frameworks.
Examine the challenges in enforcing data protection laws and the need for a robust regulatory mechanism.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of a digital lock and key overlaid with the scales of justice, symbolizing data protection and legal balance.
articles.trivia: India's data protection law, the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, was enacted after several years of deliberation and multiple draft versions.

articles.headline: Three Killed, Several Injured in Tragic Bus-Motorcycle Collision in Jalore, Rajasthan
articles.explainer:
• A devastating accident occurred in Jalore district, Rajasthan, claiming the lives of three individuals and injuring several others.
• The incident involved a collision between a sleeper bus and a motorcycle, highlighting concerns over road safety in the region.
• Local authorities have initiated an investigation into the cause of the accident, with preliminary reports suggesting potential factors like speeding or driver fatigue.
• The tragedy underscores the urgent need for stricter enforcement of traffic regulations and improved road infrastructure across the state.
• Victims have been identified, and efforts are underway to provide medical aid to the injured and support to the affected families.
gsSubTopics: Disaster and disaster management.

Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security; money-laundering and its prevention.
Various security forces and agencies and their mandate.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multi-faceted challenges India faces in ensuring road safety and suggest comprehensive measures to reduce road accidents.
Analyze the role of various stakeholders, including government, law enforcement, vehicle manufacturers, and civil society, in improving road safety standards.
Examine how technological interventions and infrastructure upgrades can contribute to preventing such tragic incidents.
articles.imageDescription: Emergency services attending to an accident site on a highway in a rural setting.
articles.trivia: India accounts for about 11% of the total global road accident deaths, despite having only 1% of the world's vehicles.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Inflation Concerns
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) concluded its bi-monthly meeting, deciding to maintain the repo rate at its current level for the fifth consecutive time.
• The decision comes amidst persistent global inflationary pressures and a watchful eye on domestic food inflation, which remains elevated.
• RBI Governor highlighted the need for continued vigilance to anchor inflation expectations while supporting economic growth.
• The MPC reiterated its commitment to withdraw accommodation gradually to ensure inflation aligns with the target while supporting growth.
• Analysts expect this stance to continue for the short term, with any rate cuts contingent on a significant and sustained moderation in inflation.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.

Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation systems storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.
Economics of animal-rearing.
prelimsPoints: The current repo rate (specific value to be assumed, e.g., 6.5%) was maintained.
The MPC consists of six members: three from RBI and three external members appointed by the government.
The primary objective of monetary policy in India is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
Inflation target is set at 4% with a band of +/- 2%.
mainsPoints: Discuss the challenges faced by the RBI in managing inflation while supporting economic growth in the current global economic scenario.
Analyze the impact of the RBI's 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance on different sectors of the Indian economy.
Evaluate the effectiveness of the Monetary Policy Committee in achieving its dual mandate of price stability and growth.
Examine the role of supply-side factors, particularly food inflation, in influencing the RBI's monetary policy decisions.
articles.imageDescription: The Reserve Bank of India headquarters building in Mumbai, with financial charts in the foreground.
articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was constituted in September 2016 and is mandated to meet at least four times a year.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Advanced Ocean Observation Satellite 'Oceansat-4'
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched 'Oceansat-4', an advanced earth observation satellite dedicated to studying oceanography and coastal zone management.
• The PSLV-C58 rocket carried Oceansat-4 into its designated polar sun-synchronous orbit from the Satish Dhawan Space Centre, Sriharikota.
• Oceansat-4 is equipped with high-resolution sensors to monitor ocean color, sea surface temperature, and wind vectors, crucial for marine weather forecasting and fisheries.
• This mission reinforces India's capabilities in remote sensing and its commitment to utilizing space technology for environmental monitoring and sustainable development.
• Data from Oceansat-4 will be vital for climate change studies, disaster management, and supporting the Blue Economy initiatives.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: Oceansat-4 is the latest in the Oceansat series of satellites for oceanographic studies.
It was launched using the Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle (PSLV-C58).
Primary applications include monitoring ocean primary productivity, understanding marine ecosystems, and aiding in disaster warning.
Satish Dhawan Space Centre (SDSC) in Sriharikota is India's primary spaceport.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of India's space program, particularly in earth observation, for national development and environmental monitoring.
Analyze how data from satellites like Oceansat-4 can contribute to India's Blue Economy initiatives and enhance disaster management capabilities.
Evaluate ISRO's role in advancing indigenous space technology and its contributions to global scientific endeavors.
articles.imageDescription: A PSLV rocket launching from Sriharikota, with the Earth and oceans visible in the background.
articles.trivia: ISRO's first dedicated ocean observation satellite, Oceansat-1, was launched in 1999.

articles.headline: India and ASEAN Deepen Cooperation on Digital Economy and Cybersecurity
articles.explainer:
• India and the Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN) concluded a high-level dialogue, reaffirming their commitment to expanding cooperation in the digital economy and cybersecurity.
• The discussions focused on developing common standards for digital payments, fostering cross-border data flows with privacy safeguards, and enhancing cybersecurity frameworks.
• This partnership aims to leverage India's digital public infrastructure (DPI) model to support ASEAN member states in their digital transformation journeys.
• Key agreements were reached on joint research and development in emerging technologies like AI and blockchain, and capacity building programs for cybersecurity professionals.
• The initiative is seen as crucial for promoting economic resilience and inclusive growth across the Indo-Pacific region.

Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.

prelimsPoints: ASEAN comprises 10 member states in Southeast Asia.
India's 'Act East Policy' is the framework for its engagement with ASEAN.

articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and ASEAN member states displayed together, with a digital network overlay.
articles.trivia: India became a Sectoral Dialogue Partner of ASEAN in 1992, elevating to a Summit-level Partner in 2002 and Strategic Partner in 2012.

articles.headline: Government Unveils Comprehensive National Policy on Electronic Waste Management
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change today launched a new National Policy on Electronic Waste (E-Waste) Management, aiming for a circular economy approach.
• The policy introduces stricter Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) norms, mandating manufacturers to take greater responsibility for the end-of-life management of their products.
• It emphasizes formalization of the e-waste recycling sector, promoting environmentally sound practices and ensuring worker safety.
• The policy also includes provisions for public awareness campaigns and incentives for consumers to dispose of e-waste responsibly.
• This move is critical as India faces a rapidly growing e-waste problem, posing significant environmental and health hazards.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Disaster and disaster management.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: India is among the top e-waste generating countries globally.
The policy aims to increase the collection and recycling rates of e-waste.
EPR is a key principle, making producers responsible for the entire life cycle of their products.
Informal recycling often leads to environmental pollution and health risks due to crude methods.
mainsPoints: Discuss the environmental and socio-economic challenges posed by India's burgeoning e-waste problem.
Analyze the effectiveness of the new National Policy on E-Waste Management, particularly its EPR framework, in promoting a circular economy.
Suggest additional measures or technological innovations required to improve e-waste collection, recycling, and safe disposal in India.
Examine the role of public awareness and consumer behavior in successful e-waste management.
articles.imageDescription: A pile of discarded electronic devices, symbolizing the e-waste problem, with a green leaf overlay.
articles.trivia: E-waste contains valuable materials like gold, silver, and copper, but also toxic substances such as lead, mercury, and cadmium.

articles.headline: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission Expands Scope, Integrates Telemedicine Services
articles.explainer:
• The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) today announced a significant expansion of its services, integrating advanced telemedicine platforms across the country.
• This integration aims to enhance accessibility to healthcare, especially in remote and underserved areas, by enabling virtual consultations and digital health records.
• The expanded mission will also focus on developing a unified health interface, allowing seamless sharing of patient data with consent, promoting interoperability.
• Training programs for healthcare professionals on digital tools and telemedicine protocols are being rolled out to ensure effective implementation.
• This move is a step towards achieving universal health coverage and building a robust, digitally-enabled healthcare ecosystem in India.

Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.

prelimsPoints: ABDM aims to create a digital health ecosystem.
A key component is the unique Health ID for every citizen.
Telemedicine allows remote healthcare services using communication technology.
The mission is under the National Health Authority (NHA).
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of digital health initiatives like ABDM and telemedicine in addressing healthcare disparities in India.

Examine the role of public-private partnerships in scaling up digital health solutions in India.
articles.imageDescription: A doctor consulting a patient virtually on a tablet, with a digital network background.
articles.trivia: The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission was launched nationwide in September 2021.

articles.headline: India Accelerates Border Infrastructure Development in Eastern Ladakh
articles.explainer:
• India has significantly ramped up its border infrastructure development projects in Eastern Ladakh, focusing on strategic roads, bridges, and tunnels.
• This accelerated pace is a direct response to geopolitical realities and aims to enhance troop mobility and logistics support for forward areas.
• The projects include all-weather connectivity, improving access to remote outposts and reducing travel time for military convoys.
• Advanced construction techniques and indigenous technologies are being employed to overcome challenging high-altitude terrain and extreme weather conditions.
• The development is crucial for bolstering national security and ensuring rapid deployment capabilities along the Line of Actual Control (LAC).
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: Ladakh is a strategically important Union Territory.
The Border Roads Organisation (BRO) is responsible for building and maintaining border roads.
Projects include tunnels like the Zoji La Tunnel and bridges over challenging river terrains.
Improved infrastructure reduces logistical challenges and response times for the armed forces.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of border infrastructure development in enhancing India's national security, particularly in sensitive regions like Ladakh.
Analyze the challenges involved in constructing infrastructure in high-altitude, rugged terrain and the innovative solutions being adopted.
Examine the geopolitical implications of India's accelerated infrastructure development along its northern borders.
Evaluate the role of organizations like BRO in strengthening India's defense preparedness.
articles.imageDescription: A newly constructed strategic road winding through a mountainous, snow-capped landscape in Ladakh.
articles.trivia: The Zoji La Tunnel, once completed, will provide all-weather connectivity between Srinagar, Kargil, and Leh.

articles.headline: Archaeologists Unearth Ancient Buddhist Monastery Complex in Odisha
articles.explainer:
• An archaeological excavation in Odisha has led to the discovery of a well-preserved ancient Buddhist monastery complex, dating back to the 7th-8th century CE.
• The site includes stupas, viharas (monastic cells), and intricate sculptures, offering new insights into the spread of Buddhism in eastern India.
• Researchers believe the monastery was a significant center for Buddhist learning and cultural exchange, potentially linked to the larger Kalinga region's historical Buddhist heritage.
• The discovery highlights the rich and diverse cultural history of Odisha, often overshadowed by its prominent Hindu temple architecture.
• Conservation efforts are being planned to protect the site and further research is expected to shed more light on its historical context and significance.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.

History of the world will include events from 18th century such as industrial revolution, world wars, redrawal of national boundaries, colonization, decolonization, political philosophies like communism, capitalism, socialism etc.- their forms and effect on the society.
prelimsPoints: Odisha has a rich Buddhist heritage, with sites like Ratnagiri, Udayagiri, and Lalitgiri (the 'Diamond Triangle').
Stupas are dome-shaped structures containing Buddhist relics.
Viharas are Buddhist monasteries where monks reside.
The Kalinga region was historically significant for the spread of Buddhism after Emperor Ashoka's conversion.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of recent archaeological discoveries in re-evaluating the history and spread of Buddhism in Eastern India.
Analyze the architectural and sculptural features of ancient Buddhist monasteries and their role in promoting art and culture.
Examine the challenges in preserving and promoting newly discovered archaeological sites in India.
Evaluate the potential of such discoveries in boosting cultural tourism and understanding India's syncretic heritage.
articles.imageDescription: Excavated ruins of an ancient Buddhist monastery with stupas and brick structures.
articles.trivia: The 'Diamond Triangle' of Odisha (Ratnagiri, Udayagiri, and Lalitgiri) are major Buddhist sites known for their monastic complexes.

articles.headline: Government Announces New Minimum Support Prices for Kharif Crops
articles.explainer:
• The Cabinet Committee on Economic Affairs (CCEA) today approved the Minimum Support Prices (MSP) for all mandated Kharif crops for the upcoming marketing season.
• The announcement reflects the government's commitment to ensuring remunerative prices for farmers and encouraging crop diversification.
• MSPs for key crops like paddy, pulses, and oilseeds have seen a significant hike, aiming to cover production costs and provide a reasonable profit margin for farmers.
• The decision is based on the recommendations of the Commission for Agricultural Costs and Prices (CACP) and takes into account the 'A2+FL' formula for cost calculation.
• This move is expected to boost agricultural income, stabilize rural demand, and address concerns over agrarian distress.
gsSubTopics: Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation systems storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.

Issues related to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices; Public Distribution System- objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security; Technology missions; economics of animal-rearing.
Food processing and related industries in India- scope’ and significance, location, upstream and downstream requirements, supply chain management.
keyTerms: Commission for Agricultural Costs and Prices (CACP)
prelimsPoints: Kharif crops are monsoon crops, sown in June-July and harvested in September-October.
MSP is announced for 22 mandated crops and Fair and Remunerative Price (FRP) for sugarcane.
The A2+FL cost includes paid-out costs (A2) plus the imputed value of family labor (FL).
The CCEA is chaired by the Prime Minister.
mainsPoints: Discuss the objectives and challenges associated with the Minimum Support Price (MSP) regime in India, particularly for Kharif crops.
Analyze the impact of MSP hikes on farmers' income, agricultural diversification, and food inflation.
Evaluate the recommendations of the CACP and the effectiveness of the 'A2+FL' formula in ensuring remunerative prices.
Examine the broader implications of MSP policy on government finances, food security, and international trade.
articles.imageDescription: A farmer standing in a lush green paddy field, symbolizing Kharif crops.
articles.trivia: The MSP system was introduced in the mid-1960s to support farmers and boost food production during the Green Revolution.

articles.headline: High Court Upholds Constitutional Validity of Reservation Policy in Local Body Elections
articles.explainer:
• A state High Court today delivered a crucial judgment, upholding the constitutional validity of the reservation policy implemented for Scheduled Castes, Scheduled Tribes, and Other Backward Classes in local body elections.
• The ruling dismissed several petitions challenging the quantum and methodology of reservations, stating that they align with constitutional provisions and judicial precedents.
• The court emphasized the importance of affirmative action to ensure adequate representation of marginalized communities in grassroots democratic institutions.
• While upholding the principle, the court also directed the state election commission to ensure adherence to the 'triple test' criteria laid down by the Supreme Court for OBC reservations.
• This judgment is expected to pave the way for timely conduct of forthcoming municipal and panchayat elections.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Panchayat Raj.
Statutory, regulatory and various quasi-judicial bodies.
prelimsPoints: Reservations in local bodies are mandated by Articles 243D (Panchayats) and 243T (Municipalities).

The 'triple test' for OBC reservation includes: 1) setting up a commission to inquire into the nature and implications of backwardness, 2) specifying the proportion of reservation, 3) ensuring overall reservation does not exceed 50%.
The 73rd and 74th Constitutional Amendment Acts provided for reservations for SCs/STs and women in local bodies.
High Courts have jurisdiction under Article 226/227 to hear such petitions.
mainsPoints: Discuss the constitutional rationale behind reservations in local self-government institutions and their role in promoting inclusive democracy.
Analyze the 'triple test' criteria for OBC reservations and the challenges in its implementation across various states.
Evaluate the socio-political impact of reservation policies on local governance and the representation of marginalized communities.
Examine the ongoing debates and judicial interventions regarding the extent and modalities of reservation in India.
articles.imageDescription: Gavel and law books, symbolizing a court judgment, with a backdrop of a local government building.
articles.trivia: The 73rd and 74th Constitutional Amendment Acts were enacted in 1992, giving constitutional status to Panchayati Raj Institutions and Urban Local Bodies, respectively.

articles.headline: India's National Quantum Mission Reports Significant Progress in Quantum Computing
articles.explainer:
• The Department of Science & Technology announced significant advancements under India's National Quantum Mission (NQM), particularly in the development of quantum computing prototypes.
• Researchers have successfully demonstrated stable qubit entanglement and improved quantum gate fidelities in experimental setups.
• The mission aims to develop quantum technologies for computing, communication, sensing, and metrology, positioning India as a global leader in this frontier area.
• Collaborations between academic institutions, industry partners, and government labs are driving the progress, focusing on indigenous hardware and software development.
• This progress is crucial for future applications in cryptography, drug discovery, and advanced materials science, promising transformative impacts.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: The National Quantum Mission was approved in 2023 with a budget of ₹6,000 crore.
Qubits are the basic units of quantum information, analogous to bits in classical computing.
Quantum entanglement and superposition are key principles of quantum mechanics exploited in quantum computing.
Quantum technologies have potential applications in secure communication, drug design, and climate modeling.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential transformative impact of quantum technologies on various sectors, including defense, healthcare, and finance.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities for India in becoming a global leader in quantum technology through the National Quantum Mission.
Examine the ethical and security implications of quantum computing, particularly concerning cryptography and data privacy.
Evaluate the importance of fostering collaboration between academia, industry, and government for successful R&D in cutting-edge technologies.
articles.imageDescription: An abstract depiction of quantum bits (qubits) interacting in a complex network, symbolizing quantum computing.
articles.trivia: Unlike classical bits that are either 0 or 1, a qubit can be 0, 1, or both simultaneously through superposition.

articles.headline: India Advocates for Equitable Climate Finance at UN Preparatory Meet
articles.explainer:
• India participated actively in the United Nations preparatory meeting for the upcoming COP30, strongly advocating for equitable climate finance and technology transfer to developing nations.
• The Indian delegation highlighted the historical responsibility of developed countries and emphasized the need for them to meet their financial commitments under the Paris Agreement.
• Discussions focused on setting ambitious but realistic targets for greenhouse gas emission reductions and adapting to climate change impacts.
• India also pushed for greater recognition of sustainable lifestyles and consumption patterns as part of global climate action strategies.
• The outcome of this preparatory meet will shape the agenda and negotiations for the crucial COP30 summit.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.

prelimsPoints: COP30 is scheduled for 2025 (location to be determined, or assume a general location if not specific).
The Paris Agreement aims to limit global warming to well below 2, preferably to 1.5 degrees Celsius.
Developed nations pledged to mobilize $100 billion per year by 2020 for climate action in developing countries, a target often unmet.

mainsPoints: Discuss India's consistent stance on climate justice and equitable burden-sharing at international climate forums.

Examine how India's domestic climate policies align with its international commitments and advocacy.
articles.imageDescription: A global map highlighting India, with symbols of renewable energy and climate negotiations.
articles.trivia: The first Conference of Parties (COP1) to the UNFCCC took place in Berlin, Germany, in 1995.

articles.headline: Project Tiger Unveils New Conservation Strategies to Combat Poaching and Habitat Loss
articles.explainer:
• Project Tiger, India's flagship tiger conservation program, announced a set of new strategies focused on advanced anti-poaching measures and community-based habitat protection.
• The new approach includes enhanced surveillance technologies, increased deployment of forest guards, and intelligence-led operations to disrupt poaching networks.
• Emphasis is also placed on restoring critical tiger corridors, mitigating human-wildlife conflict, and engaging local communities as active partners in conservation efforts.
• The initiative aims to address emerging threats like climate change impacts on tiger habitats and the increasing fragmentation of forests.
• India, home to over 70% of the world's wild tiger population, continues to lead global efforts in tiger conservation.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Biodiversity and Environment.
prelimsPoints: Project Tiger was launched in 1973.
India has over 50 tiger reserves.
The latest tiger census (2022) reported 3,682 tigers in India.
M-STrIPES (Monitoring System for Tigers – Intensive Protection and Ecological Status) is a software-based monitoring system for tiger reserves.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolution and success of Project Tiger in India, highlighting its key achievements and the challenges it continues to face.
Analyze the effectiveness of new conservation strategies, including technological interventions and community involvement, in combating poaching and habitat loss.

articles.imageDescription: A majestic tiger walking through dense forest foliage, symbolizing wildlife conservation.
articles.trivia: The Bengal Tiger is India's national animal and is listed as 'Endangered' on the IUCN Red List.

articles.headline: Government Launches New Initiatives to Boost Women's Entrepreneurship and Self-Reliance
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Women and Child Development, in collaboration with NITI Aayog, launched a series of new initiatives aimed at fostering women's entrepreneurship and self-reliance.
• The programs include enhanced access to credit, mentorship networks, skill development workshops, and market linkages for women-led businesses.
• A dedicated digital portal has been unveiled to provide a one-stop platform for aspiring women entrepreneurs to access government schemes, resources, and expert advice.
• The focus is on empowering women in both traditional and emerging sectors, promoting financial independence and contributing to economic growth.
• These initiatives align with the broader vision of 'Nari Shakti' and inclusive development.

articles.headline: Government Releases Latest 'Ease of Doing Business' State Rankings, Gujarat Tops Chart
articles.explainer:
• The Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT) today released its latest 'Ease of Doing Business' (EoDB) State Rankings, with Gujarat securing the top position.
• The rankings assess states based on the implementation of business reforms, including ease of obtaining permits, labor regulations, and environmental clearances.
• The exercise aims to foster a competitive environment among states to attract investment and promote economic growth.
• Several states showed significant improvement, indicating a positive trend in streamlining regulatory processes and reducing compliance burdens for businesses.
• The report provides valuable insights for policymakers to identify areas for further reform and enhance India's overall business environment.

Government Budgeting.
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth.

articles.headline: DRDO and Safran Collaborate on Indigenous Fighter Jet Engine Development
articles.explainer:
• The Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO) and French aerospace major Safran have formalized a joint venture to develop a high-thrust indigenous fighter jet engine.
• This collaboration aims to significantly reduce India's reliance on imported engines for its next-generation fighter aircraft programs, including the AMCA (Advanced Medium Combat Aircraft).
• The agreement involves extensive technology transfer, co-development, and co-production, ensuring India gains critical expertise in advanced jet engine technology.
• The project is a major step towards 'Aatmanirbhar Bharat' in defense manufacturing and enhancing India's strategic autonomy.
• Successful development will position India among a select few nations capable of designing and producing advanced fighter aircraft engines.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.

articles.headline: India Inaugurates Mega Solar Energy Park in Rajasthan, Boosting Renewable Capacity
articles.explainer:
• India today inaugurated a new mega solar energy park in Rajasthan, adding significantly to the nation's renewable energy generation capacity.
• The park, developed through a public-private partnership, features advanced photovoltaic technology and smart grid integration.
• This project is part of India's ambitious target to achieve 500 GW of non-fossil fuel energy capacity by 2030.
• Rajasthan's abundant solar insolation makes it an ideal location for large-scale solar power projects, contributing to green energy transition.
• The initiative is expected to create local employment opportunities and reduce carbon emissions, aligning with India's climate commitments.

articles.headline: Startup India Seed Fund Scheme Shows Positive Impact on Emerging Startups
articles.explainer:
• The Startup India Seed Fund Scheme (SISFS) has demonstrated significant success in nurturing early-stage startups across various sectors, according to a recent government review.
• The scheme provides financial assistance to startups for proof of concept, prototype development, product trials, and market-entry.
• It has particularly benefited startups in Tier 2 and Tier 3 cities, fostering innovation and entrepreneurship beyond major metropolitan areas.
• The review highlighted the scheme's role in creating a robust startup ecosystem and facilitating access to crucial early-stage capital, which is often a major hurdle.
• This success reinforces India's position as the third-largest startup ecosystem globally, with a focus on inclusive growth.

articles.headline: India and Pakistan Hold Technical Discussions on Indus Waters Treaty
articles.explainer:
• India and Pakistan held technical discussions under the framework of the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) in New Delhi, addressing various aspects of water sharing and project designs.
• The meeting focused on resolving technical disputes concerning hydroelectric projects being constructed by India on the Western rivers.
• Both sides reiterated their commitment to upholding the provisions of the IWT, which has governed water distribution between the two nations for over six decades.
• While no major breakthroughs were reported, the ongoing dialogue is seen as crucial for maintaining stability and cooperation on transboundary water issues.
• Experts emphasize the importance of continued engagement to prevent escalation of water-related disputes.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the recent Supreme Court judgment on Data Protection and Right to Privacy, consider the following statements:
1. The judgment primarily re-emphasizes the principles laid down in the Puttaswamy judgment (2017).
2. It provides specific guidelines for state surveillance, mandating proportionality and legal backing for any intrusion.
3. The ruling directly amends the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, to incorporate new provisions.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct. The judgment builds upon and re-emphasizes the principles of the Right to Privacy established by the Puttaswamy judgment. Statement 2 is correct. The ruling sets guidelines for state intrusion, emphasizing proportionality and legal basis. Statement 3 is incorrect. Supreme Court judgments interpret laws and the Constitution; they do not directly amend acts. While the ruling will influence the implementation and potential future amendments, it does not directly amend the Act.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following statements regarding the Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is/are correct?
1. The MPC is chaired by the Union Finance Minister.
2. Its primary objective is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
3. The MPC has six members, with an equal number from RBI and external members appointed by the government.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect. The MPC is chaired by the RBI Governor. Statement 2 is correct. The primary objective is price stability, considering growth. Statement 3 is correct. The MPC comprises three RBI officials (Governor, Deputy Governor, and one other officer) and three external members appointed by the government.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding India's space missions:
1. Oceansat-4 is an advanced communication satellite launched by ISRO.
2. The Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle (PSLV) is primarily used for placing satellites into geostationary orbits.
3. Data from ocean observation satellites are crucial for the Blue Economy and climate change studies.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect. Oceansat-4 is an earth observation satellite dedicated to studying oceanography, not primarily communication. Statement 2 is incorrect. PSLV is primarily used for placing satellites into polar sun-synchronous orbits, while GSLV is used for geostationary orbits. Statement 3 is correct. Ocean observation data is vital for marine resource management (Blue Economy) and monitoring climate change impacts on oceans.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following is/are the key principle(s) of the new National Policy on Electronic Waste (E-Waste) Management?
1. Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR)
2. Formalization of the recycling sector
3. Promotion of incineration for waste disposal
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statements 1 and 2 are correct. The new policy emphasizes stricter EPR norms, making producers responsible for end-of-life management, and aims to formalize the e-waste recycling sector to ensure environmentally sound practices. Statement 3 is incorrect. Incineration is generally not promoted for e-waste due to the release of toxic substances; the policy focuses on recycling and safe disposal methods.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM), consider the following statements:
1. It aims to create a nationwide digital health infrastructure.
2. A key component is the provision of a unique Health ID for every citizen.
3. The mission exclusively focuses on providing health insurance coverage to the poor.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct. ABDM aims to build a comprehensive digital health ecosystem. Statement 2 is correct. The generation of a unique Health ID (now ABHA - Ayushman Bharat Health Account) is a foundational element. Statement 3 is incorrect. While Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (AB PM-JAY) focuses on health insurance, ABDM's scope is broader, encompassing digital health records, telemedicine, and health registries, not exclusively insurance.

GS1.headline: Archaeologists Unearth Ancient Buddhist Monastery Complex in Odisha
GS1.explainer:
• An archaeological excavation in Odisha has led to the discovery of a well-preserved ancient Buddhist monastery complex, dating back to the 7th-8th century CE.
• The site includes stupas, viharas (monastic cells), and intricate sculptures, offering new insights into the spread of Buddhism in eastern India.
• Researchers believe the monastery was a significant center for Buddhist learning and cultural exchange, potentially linked to the larger Kalinga region's historical Buddhist heritage.
• The discovery highlights the rich and diverse cultural history of Odisha, often overshadowed by its prominent Hindu temple architecture.
• Conservation efforts are being planned to protect the site and further research is expected to shed more light on its historical context and significance.

History of the world will include events from 18th century such as industrial revolution, world wars, redrawal of national boundaries, colonization, decolonization, political philosophies like communism, capitalism, socialism etc.- their forms and effect on the society.
prelimsPoints: Odisha has a rich Buddhist heritage, with sites like Ratnagiri, Udayagiri, and Lalitgiri (the 'Diamond Triangle').
Stupas are dome-shaped structures containing Buddhist relics.
Viharas are Buddhist monasteries where monks reside.
The Kalinga region was historically significant for the spread of Buddhism after Emperor Ashoka's conversion.

Analyze the architectural and sculptural features of ancient Buddhist monasteries and their role in promoting art and culture.

GS1.headline: Government Launches New Initiatives to Boost Women's Entrepreneurship and Self-Reliance
GS1.explainer:
• The Ministry of Women and Child Development, in collaboration with NITI Aayog, launched a series of new initiatives aimed at fostering women's entrepreneurship and self-reliance.
• The programs include enhanced access to credit, mentorship networks, skill development workshops, and market linkages for women-led businesses.
• A dedicated digital portal has been unveiled to provide a one-stop platform for aspiring women entrepreneurs to access government schemes, resources, and expert advice.
• The focus is on empowering women in both traditional and emerging sectors, promoting financial independence and contributing to economic growth.
• These initiatives align with the broader vision of 'Nari Shakti' and inclusive development.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Delivers Landmark Ruling on Data Protection and Digital Privacy
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India today delivered a significant judgment clarifying the contours of the Right to Privacy in the digital age, particularly concerning the implementation of the new Data Protection Act.
• The ruling emphasizes the need for a balanced approach between state surveillance, national security, and individual privacy rights, reiterating that any state intrusion must be proportionate and based on law.
• It sets guidelines for data fiduciaries and processors regarding consent, data minimization, and purpose limitation, impacting digital service providers and government agencies alike.
• Experts believe the judgment will influence the operational aspects of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, and future legislative amendments.
• This decision is expected to strengthen India's position as a proponent of robust data governance while fostering innovation in the digital economy.

Key provisions of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, are contextualized by the ruling.

• The discussions focused on developing common standards for digital payments, fostering cross-border data flows with privacy safeguards, and enhancing cybersecurity frameworks.
• This partnership aims to leverage India's digital public infrastructure (DPI) model to support ASEAN member states in their digital transformation journeys.
• Key agreements were reached on joint research and development in emerging technologies like AI and blockchain, and capacity building programs for cybersecurity professionals.
• The initiative is seen as crucial for promoting economic resilience and inclusive growth across the Indo-Pacific region.

• Training programs for healthcare professionals on digital tools and telemedicine protocols are being rolled out to ensure effective implementation.

GS2.headline: High Court Upholds Constitutional Validity of Reservation Policy in Local Body Elections
GS2.explainer:
• A state High Court today delivered a crucial judgment, upholding the constitutional validity of the reservation policy implemented for Scheduled Castes, Scheduled Tribes, and Other Backward Classes in local body elections.
• The ruling dismissed several petitions challenging the quantum and methodology of reservations, stating that they align with constitutional provisions and judicial precedents.
• The court emphasized the importance of affirmative action to ensure adequate representation of marginalized communities in grassroots democratic institutions.
• While upholding the principle, the court also directed the state election commission to ensure adherence to the 'triple test' criteria laid down by the Supreme Court for OBC reservations.
• This judgment is expected to pave the way for timely conduct of forthcoming municipal and panchayat elections.

The 'triple test' for OBC reservation includes: 1) setting up a commission to inquire into the nature and implications of backwardness, 2) specifying the proportion of reservation, 3) ensuring overall reservation does not exceed 50%.

GS3.headline: Three Killed, Several Injured in Tragic Bus-Motorcycle Collision in Jalore, Rajasthan
GS3.explainer:
• A devastating accident occurred in Jalore district, Rajasthan, claiming the lives of three individuals and injuring several others.
• The incident involved a collision between a sleeper bus and a motorcycle, highlighting concerns over road safety in the region.
• Local authorities have initiated an investigation into the cause of the accident, with preliminary reports suggesting potential factors like speeding or driver fatigue.
• The tragedy underscores the urgent need for stricter enforcement of traffic regulations and improved road infrastructure across the state.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Inflation Concerns
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) concluded its bi-monthly meeting, deciding to maintain the repo rate at its current level for the fifth consecutive time.
• The decision comes amidst persistent global inflationary pressures and a watchful eye on domestic food inflation, which remains elevated.
• RBI Governor highlighted the need for continued vigilance to anchor inflation expectations while supporting economic growth.
• The MPC reiterated its commitment to withdraw accommodation gradually to ensure inflation aligns with the target while supporting growth.
• Analysts expect this stance to continue for the short term, with any rate cuts contingent on a significant and sustained moderation in inflation.

Government Budgeting.

The primary objective of monetary policy in India is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
Inflation target is set at 4% with a band of +/- 2%.

GS3.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was constituted in September 2016 and is mandated to meet at least four times a year.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Launches Advanced Ocean Observation Satellite 'Oceansat-4'
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched 'Oceansat-4', an advanced earth observation satellite dedicated to studying oceanography and coastal zone management.
• The PSLV-C58 rocket carried Oceansat-4 into its designated polar sun-synchronous orbit from the Satish Dhawan Space Centre, Sriharikota.
• Oceansat-4 is equipped with high-resolution sensors to monitor ocean color, sea surface temperature, and wind vectors, crucial for marine weather forecasting and fisheries.
• This mission reinforces India's capabilities in remote sensing and its commitment to utilizing space technology for environmental monitoring and sustainable development.
• Data from Oceansat-4 will be vital for climate change studies, disaster management, and supporting the Blue Economy initiatives.

prelimsPoints: Oceansat-4 is the latest in the Oceansat series of satellites for oceanographic studies.

GS3.imageDescription: A PSLV rocket launching from Sriharikota, with the Earth and oceans visible in the background.
GS3.trivia: ISRO's first dedicated ocean observation satellite, Oceansat-1, was launched in 1999.

GS3.headline: Government Unveils Comprehensive National Policy on Electronic Waste Management
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change today launched a new National Policy on Electronic Waste (E-Waste) Management, aiming for a circular economy approach.
• The policy introduces stricter Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) norms, mandating manufacturers to take greater responsibility for the end-of-life management of their products.
• It emphasizes formalization of the e-waste recycling sector, promoting environmentally sound practices and ensuring worker safety.
• The policy also includes provisions for public awareness campaigns and incentives for consumers to dispose of e-waste responsibly.
• This move is critical as India faces a rapidly growing e-waste problem, posing significant environmental and health hazards.

prelimsPoints: India is among the top e-waste generating countries globally.
The policy aims to increase the collection and recycling rates of e-waste.
EPR is a key principle, making producers responsible for the entire life cycle of their products.
Informal recycling often leads to environmental pollution and health risks due to crude methods.
mainsPoints: Discuss the environmental and socio-economic challenges posed by India's burgeoning e-waste problem.

GS3.imageDescription: A pile of discarded electronic devices, symbolizing the e-waste problem, with a green leaf overlay.
GS3.trivia: E-waste contains valuable materials like gold, silver, and copper, but also toxic substances such as lead, mercury, and cadmium.

GS3.headline: India Accelerates Border Infrastructure Development in Eastern Ladakh
GS3.explainer:
• India has significantly ramped up its border infrastructure development projects in Eastern Ladakh, focusing on strategic roads, bridges, and tunnels.
• This accelerated pace is a direct response to geopolitical realities and aims to enhance troop mobility and logistics support for forward areas.
• The projects include all-weather connectivity, improving access to remote outposts and reducing travel time for military convoys.
• Advanced construction techniques and indigenous technologies are being employed to overcome challenging high-altitude terrain and extreme weather conditions.
• The development is crucial for bolstering national security and ensuring rapid deployment capabilities along the Line of Actual Control (LAC).

The Border Roads Organisation (BRO) is responsible for building and maintaining border roads.
Projects include tunnels like the Zoji La Tunnel and bridges over challenging river terrains.
Improved infrastructure reduces logistical challenges and response times for the armed forces.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of border infrastructure development in enhancing India's national security, particularly in sensitive regions like Ladakh.
Analyze the challenges involved in constructing infrastructure in high-altitude, rugged terrain and the innovative solutions being adopted.

GS3.trivia: The Zoji La Tunnel, once completed, will provide all-weather connectivity between Srinagar, Kargil, and Leh.

GS3.headline: Government Announces New Minimum Support Prices for Kharif Crops
GS3.explainer:
• The Cabinet Committee on Economic Affairs (CCEA) today approved the Minimum Support Prices (MSP) for all mandated Kharif crops for the upcoming marketing season.
• The announcement reflects the government's commitment to ensuring remunerative prices for farmers and encouraging crop diversification.
• MSPs for key crops like paddy, pulses, and oilseeds have seen a significant hike, aiming to cover production costs and provide a reasonable profit margin for farmers.
• The decision is based on the recommendations of the Commission for Agricultural Costs and Prices (CACP) and takes into account the 'A2+FL' formula for cost calculation.
• This move is expected to boost agricultural income, stabilize rural demand, and address concerns over agrarian distress.

GS3.trivia: The MSP system was introduced in the mid-1960s to support farmers and boost food production during the Green Revolution.

GS3.headline: India's National Quantum Mission Reports Significant Progress in Quantum Computing
GS3.explainer:
• The Department of Science & Technology announced significant advancements under India's National Quantum Mission (NQM), particularly in the development of quantum computing prototypes.
• Researchers have successfully demonstrated stable qubit entanglement and improved quantum gate fidelities in experimental setups.
• The mission aims to develop quantum technologies for computing, communication, sensing, and metrology, positioning India as a global leader in this frontier area.
• Collaborations between academic institutions, industry partners, and government labs are driving the progress, focusing on indigenous hardware and software development.
• This progress is crucial for future applications in cryptography, drug discovery, and advanced materials science, promising transformative impacts.

GS3.headline: Project Tiger Unveils New Conservation Strategies to Combat Poaching and Habitat Loss
GS3.explainer:
• Project Tiger, India's flagship tiger conservation program, announced a set of new strategies focused on advanced anti-poaching measures and community-based habitat protection.
• The new approach includes enhanced surveillance technologies, increased deployment of forest guards, and intelligence-led operations to disrupt poaching networks.
• Emphasis is also placed on restoring critical tiger corridors, mitigating human-wildlife conflict, and engaging local communities as active partners in conservation efforts.
• The initiative aims to address emerging threats like climate change impacts on tiger habitats and the increasing fragmentation of forests.
• India, home to over 70% of the world's wild tiger population, continues to lead global efforts in tiger conservation.

GS3.trivia: The Bengal Tiger is India's national animal and is listed as 'Endangered' on the IUCN Red List.

GS3.headline: Government Releases Latest 'Ease of Doing Business' State Rankings, Gujarat Tops Chart
GS3.explainer:
• The Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT) today released its latest 'Ease of Doing Business' (EoDB) State Rankings, with Gujarat securing the top position.
• The rankings assess states based on the implementation of business reforms, including ease of obtaining permits, labor regulations, and environmental clearances.
• The exercise aims to foster a competitive environment among states to attract investment and promote economic growth.
• Several states showed significant improvement, indicating a positive trend in streamlining regulatory processes and reducing compliance burdens for businesses.
• The report provides valuable insights for policymakers to identify areas for further reform and enhance India's overall business environment.

GS3.headline: DRDO and Safran Collaborate on Indigenous Fighter Jet Engine Development
GS3.explainer:
• The Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO) and French aerospace major Safran have formalized a joint venture to develop a high-thrust indigenous fighter jet engine.
• This collaboration aims to significantly reduce India's reliance on imported engines for its next-generation fighter aircraft programs, including the AMCA (Advanced Medium Combat Aircraft).
• The agreement involves extensive technology transfer, co-development, and co-production, ensuring India gains critical expertise in advanced jet engine technology.
• The project is a major step towards 'Aatmanirbhar Bharat' in defense manufacturing and enhancing India's strategic autonomy.
• Successful development will position India among a select few nations capable of designing and producing advanced fighter aircraft engines.

GS3.headline: India Inaugurates Mega Solar Energy Park in Rajasthan, Boosting Renewable Capacity
GS3.explainer:
• India today inaugurated a new mega solar energy park in Rajasthan, adding significantly to the nation's renewable energy generation capacity.
• The park, developed through a public-private partnership, features advanced photovoltaic technology and smart grid integration.
• This project is part of India's ambitious target to achieve 500 GW of non-fossil fuel energy capacity by 2030.
• Rajasthan's abundant solar insolation makes it an ideal location for large-scale solar power projects, contributing to green energy transition.
• The initiative is expected to create local employment opportunities and reduce carbon emissions, aligning with India's climate commitments.

GS3.headline: Startup India Seed Fund Scheme Shows Positive Impact on Emerging Startups
GS3.explainer:
• The Startup India Seed Fund Scheme (SISFS) has demonstrated significant success in nurturing early-stage startups across various sectors, according to a recent government review.
• The scheme provides financial assistance to startups for proof of concept, prototype development, product trials, and market-entry.
• It has particularly benefited startups in Tier 2 and Tier 3 cities, fostering innovation and entrepreneurship beyond major metropolitan areas.
• The review highlighted the scheme's role in creating a robust startup ecosystem and facilitating access to crucial early-stage capital, which is often a major hurdle.
• This success reinforces India's position as the third-largest startup ecosystem globally, with a focus on inclusive growth.
